name: ryutils

metadata.name: ryutils
metadata.summary: A collection of utilities for Python

documentation:
# My Common Python Utils

[![Python application](https://github.com/droneshire/my-py-utils/actions/workflows/python-app.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/droneshire/my-py-utils/actions/workflows/python-app.yml)
[![Upload Python Package](https://github.com/droneshire/my-py-utils/actions/workflows/python-publish.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/droneshire/my-py-utils/actions/workflows/python-publish.yml)

A collection of util files that I've built over time for various projects that I've built

modules.name: ryutils.csv_logger
documentation: Utility script that helps write transactions to a spreadsheet

functions.name: ryutils.fmt_util.get_pretty_seconds
documentation:
Given an amount of seconds, return a formatted string with
hours, minutes and seconds; taken from
https://stackoverflow.com/a/775075/2972183

functions.name: ryutils.web2_client.create_websocket_url
documentation: Constructs a WebSocket URL with given parameters.

functions.name: ryutils.async_utils.force_sync
documentation: Force a function to run synchronously.

classes.name: ryutils.async_utils.Worker
documentation:
Worker thread class

This module provides a class for creating a worker thread that utilizes
Queue.Queue to process messages.

functions.name: ryutils.dict_util.find_in_nested_dict
documentation: Helper function to search for a key in a nested dictionary/list structure.

functions.name: ryutils.email_util.send_email
documentation:
Given a list of emails, try to send a the email from one of the accounts.
Multiple emails helps in the case you are getting throttled from sending too many emails
from one account.

This method should not be used to send a the same email to many accounts. That should be done
outside this method by calling this method multiple times.

functions.name: ryutils.log.clean_log_dir
documentation: Clean the log directory by removing all files and directories in the directory.

functions.name: ryutils.log.tar_logs
documentation: Tar the logs directory to a file called logs.tar.gz

classes.name: ryutils.log.MultiHandler
documentation:
Create a special logger that logs to per-thread-name files
I'm not confident the locking strategy here is correct, I think this is
a global lock and it'd be OK to just have a per-thread or per-file lock.
